questions.question: Which statement about silicon( IV) oxide, SiO 2 , is correct? A It conducts electricity because it contains delocalised electrons., B It has a giant covalent structure with each silicon atom bonded to four oxygen atoms., C It is a simple covalent molecule., D Its structure is similar to that of graphite. 4
questions.text: Which statement about silicon( IV) oxide, SiO 2 , is correct? A It conducts electricity because it contains delocalised electrons., B It has a giant covalent structure with each silicon atom bonded to four oxygen atoms., C It is a simple covalent molecule., D Its structure is similar to that of graphite. 4
options: A It conducts electricity because it contains delocalised electrons.
B It has a giant covalent structure with each silicon atom bonded to four oxygen atoms.
C It is a simple covalent molecule.
D Its structure is similar to that of graphite. 4

questions.question: When 65 g of zinc reacts with 32 g of sulfur, 97 g of zinc sulfide is produced. When 65 g of zinc reacts with 40 g of sulfur, the mass of zinc sulfide produced is still 97 g. Which statement explains this observation? A Some of the zinc sulfide evaporates., B The reaction rate is slow., C The reaction stops before it is complete., D Zinc is the limiting reactant.
questions.text: When 65 g of zinc reacts with 32 g of sulfur, 97 g of zinc sulfide is produced. When 65 g of zinc reacts with 40 g of sulfur, the mass of zinc sulfide produced is still 97 g. Which statement explains this observation? A Some of the zinc sulfide evaporates., B The reaction rate is slow., C The reaction stops before it is complete., D Zinc is the limiting reactant.
options: A Some of the zinc sulfide evaporates.
B The reaction rate is slow.
C The reaction stops before it is complete.
D Zinc is the limiting reactant.

questions.question: HydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells can be used to power vehicles. Which statement about hydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells is correct? A The equation for the overall reaction is H 2 +  O 2 п‚®  H 2 O 2 ., B The only chemical products are water and carbon dioxide., C Chemical energy in the fuel is converted into electrical energy., D The hydrogen used in hydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells is a fossil fuel.
questions.text: HydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells can be used to power vehicles. Which statement about hydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells is correct? A The equation for the overall reaction is H 2 +  O 2 п‚®  H 2 O 2 ., B The only chemical products are water and carbon dioxide., C Chemical energy in the fuel is converted into electrical energy., D The hydrogen used in hydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells is a fossil fuel.
options: A The equation for the overall reaction is H 2 +  O 2 п‚®  H 2 O 2 .
B The only chemical products are water and carbon dioxide.
C Chemical energy in the fuel is converted into electrical energy.
D The hydrogen used in hydrogenвЂ“oxygen fuel cells is a fossil fuel.

questions.question: A farmer incorrectly adds two substances to the soil at the same time. They react together to form a gas which turns damp red litmus paper blue. What are the two substances? A a basic oxide and a potassium salt, B a basic oxide and an ammonium salt, C an acidic oxide and a potassium salt, D an acidic oxide and an ammonium salt 7

A a basic oxide and a potassium salt, B a basic oxide and an ammonium salt, C an acidic oxide and a potassium salt, D an acidic oxide and an ammonium salt 7
options: A a basic oxide and a potassium salt
B a basic oxide and an ammonium salt

D an acidic oxide and an ammonium salt 7

questions.question: Brass is an alloy of copper. Which statement about brass or copper is correct? A Brass does not conduct electricity because it is a compound., B Copper conducts electricity because its atoms are free to move., C Brass is harder than copper because its layers of atoms cannot easily slide over each other., D Copper is stronger than brass because it is a pure metal. 9
questions.text: Brass is an alloy of copper. Which statement about brass or copper is correct? A Brass does not conduct electricity because it is a compound., B Copper conducts electricity because its atoms are free to move., C Brass is harder than copper because its layers of atoms cannot easily slide over each other., D Copper is stronger than brass because it is a pure metal. 9
options: A Brass does not conduct electricity because it is a compound.
B Copper conducts electricity because its atoms are free to move.
C Brass is harder than copper because its layers of atoms cannot easily slide over each other.
D Copper is stronger than brass because it is a pure metal. 9

questions.question: The ionic equations for some reactions between metals and aqueous metal ions are shown. Cu  +  2Ag + п‚®  2Ag  +  Cu 2+ Mg  +  Zn 2+ п‚®  Mg 2+ +  Zn Zn  +  Cu 2+ п‚®  Cu  +  Zn 2+ Sn  +  Cu 2+ п‚®  Sn 2+ +  Cu Which further information can be used to place copper, magnesium, silver, tin and zinc in order of their reactivity? A Zinc reacts with Sn 2+ ions., B Copper does not react with Sn 2+ ions., C Magnesium reacts with Cu 2+ ions., D Magnesium reacts with Ag + ions.
questions.text: The ionic equations for some reactions between metals and aqueous metal ions are shown. A Zinc reacts with Sn 2+ ions., B Copper does not react with Sn 2+ ions., C Magnesium reacts with Cu 2+ ions., D Magnesium reacts with Ag + ions.

B Copper does not react with Sn 2+ ions.
C Magnesium reacts with Cu 2+ ions.
D Magnesium reacts with Ag + ions.

questions.question: Zinc is used to galvanise iron to prevent it from rusting. Which statements are correct? 1   Galvanising is an example of a barrier method. 2   If the zinc is scratched, the iron will rust very quickly. 3   Galvanising is an example of sacrificial protection. 4   Zinc is more reactive than iron and so accepts electrons more readily. A 1, 2 and 4 B   1 and 3 C   2, 3 and 4 D   2 and 3 only

A 1, 2 and 4 B   1 and 3 C   2, 3 and 4 D   2 and 3 only
options: A 1, 2 and 4 B   1 and 3 C   2, 3 and 4 D   2 and 3 only

A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C   2 and 3 D   2 and 4

questions.question: Ethanol can be made by the fermentation of aqueous glucose and by the catalytic addition of steam to ethene. What are two advantages of making ethanol by the catalytic addition of steam to ethene rather than by the fermentation of aqueous glucose? A faster reaction and renewable raw materials, B purer product and faster reaction, C renewable raw materials and continuous process, D uses more energy and forms a purer product

D uses more energy and forms a purer product

questions.text: Pure ethanol has a melting point of вЂ“114 В°C and a boiling point of 78 В°C.
